CapGainsLossTxInvstIncmGrp.PropertyDesc: Blackrock US Debt Index Fund B
Blackrock US Debt Index Fund B

SummaryOfDirectChrtblActyGrp.Description1Txt: ONGOING STRATEGIC CONSULTATION, TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE, AND DATA ANALYSIS AT NO COST TO CHILD WELFARE AGENCIES, POLICY MAKERS, COURTS, AND TRIBES ACROSS AMERICA
SummaryOfDirectChrtblActyGrp.Description2Txt: Provide Direct Services to more than 1,400 youth and families THROUGH COMMUNITY-BASED OFFICES AND WE WORK COLLABORATIVELY WITH CHILD WELFARE SYSTEMS AND COURTS.
SummaryOfDirectChrtblActyGrp.Description3Txt: RESEARCH ON CHILD WELFARE PROGRAMS, PROVIDE SCHOLARSHIPS TO FOSTER YOUTH, AND EDUCATE POLICY MAKERS ABOUT CHILD WELFARE ISSUES.

GrantOrContributionPdDurYrGrp.GrantOrContributionPurposeTxt: To support Grantee's 2018 Conference, which is to take place on April 14-16, 2018, in Memphis, Tennessee.
To support Grantee's feasibility study, which is focused on increasing the operational capacity and strategic impact of Grantee's philanthropic sector.

To support Grantee's 2018 Philanthropy Institute, which is scheduled to take place on June 13-15, 2018 in Santa Ana Pueblo, New Mexico.
To support Grantee's Get-Out-Vote nonpartisan Native Voter Engagement project involving a public service announcement in several states and locations.
To support Grantee's youth-focused initiatives to elevate Native youth voices and priorities.
To support the CICW in developing the capacity of child welfare jurisdictions, community partners, and other groups by creating training resources and other educational materials.

GrantOrContributionPdDurYrGrp.GrantOrContributionPurposeTxt: To support Grantee's development of strategic plan outlines, and identification of best practices and priorities for the implementation of strategies that will result in a continued decrease in the number of children in foster care and the number of individuals overdosing from the use of opioids/heroin.

To support Northwest African American Museum in achieving its mission to spread knowledge, understanding, and enjoyment of the histories, arts and cultures of people of African descent for the enrichment of all.
To support Grantee's implementation of twice-monthly support groups for youth in King County that have been impacted by commercial sexual exploitation.
To support Grantee's development of a Scope of Research structured around advancing diversity, equity, and inclusion in the work of various organizations.
To support Grantee's Strategic Directions Task Force, and the 70th Annual Conference.
To help grantee with their mission to deeply and effectively engage families for each young person served while building their skills and community connections, and by sharing knowledge with others.
To provide relief from the impact of recent hurricanes to children and families in the care of the Administraci6n de Familias y Ninos (ADFAN), as well as to the community at large.
To support Grantee's 30th Anniversary Annual Gala, which is to take place on May 14, 2018 in New York, New York.
To support Grantee's project, Executives' Alliance for Boys and Men of Color, specifically, the 2018 Spring Meeting.
To support Grantee's Blue Ribbon Luncheon, which is scheduled to take place on April 19, 2018, in San Francisco, California.
To support the launch of Grantee's Homeless Student Campaign at the South by Southwest EDU Conference that is to take place on March 5-8, 2018, in Austin,
To support the Seattle Theater Group "AileyCamp", a free six-week camp for youth ages 11-14 that uses the power of dance to enhance and positively alter the lives of underserved youth.
